self_audit.all_keys_referenced: PASS — all 31 layout keys confirmed referenced in scene via node script
self_audit.diff_logic_clean: PASS — diff shows only position/size substitutions plus import, preload(), layoutEditorConfig, and loadLayout call

positions_left_in_code.reason: Bike spawn point is part of a tween animation sequence (load-test bike crossing the bridge). Both x=0 and y=WASH_Y-14 are the dynamic start position of a tween target, exempt per rule 7 (per-frame/dynamic positions). The tween's x target (width) is also dynamic. Extracting these would not benefit the editor — they are animation anchors driven by the bridge construction event, not static scene decorations.

positions_left_in_code.location: _renderWashChannel — jagged edge random triangles and pebble circles
positions_left_in_code.reason: Random positions computed per-call via Math.random(). Exempt per rule 7 / procedural generation. The channel anchor values (WASH_Y / WASH_HALF_HEIGHT) ARE captured in layout as wash_channel.y and wash_channel.half_height.

positions_left_in_code.location: _renderMonsoonSign(x, y) — relative offsets: y+30, y-4, y+9, w=6/60/64/36
positions_left_in_code.reason: The function takes an (x,y) anchor parameter. The offsets (+30, -4, +9) and sizes (6, 60, 64, 36) are structural proportions of the sign element, not independent positioned objects. The anchor (x=60, y=200) is captured in layout as monsoon_sign. The sub-element sizes (post: 6x60, face: 64x36) are decorative geometry dimensions, not independently positionable; leaving them in code is consistent with treating the sign as a single composited element. They can be extracted in a future pass if the editor needs per-part control.

positions_left_in_code.location: _drawGhostBeam / _drawPlacedBeam — slot.x, slot.y, w, h, glow offset slot.y - 22
positions_left_in_code.reason: These render hooks consume positions from BRIDGE_MESQUITE_BLUEPRINT.slots (a data structure belonging to the bridge blueprint, not this scene). The -22 glow offset is a fixed decorative offset within the ghost-beam primitive. These are exempt — they are blueprint-driven, not scene-layout literals.

notes: Scene: DryWashScene (900x600). 31 objects extracted to public/layouts/dry-wash.layout.json (195 lines — just under the 200-line cap).

The wash_channel object captures the module-level constants WASH_Y=400 and WASH_HALF_HEIGHT=40 which were used as anchors throughout _renderWashChannel and _renderBrokenRemnants. Both methods now read from this.layout.wash_channel.y / .half_height.
Triangle points (mesa_1/2/3, broken_stub_near_crack, broken_stub_far_crack) use x1/y1/x2/y2/x3/y3 fields since fillTriangle takes 6 positional arguments.

The WASH_Y and WASH_HALF_HEIGHT module-level constants remain in the source file as dead code after the refactor — they are no longer referenced by any code path. This is a pre-existing observation, NOT a logic change. Leaving them in place per rule 4 (no logic changes). A future cleanup pass can remove them.
layoutEditorConfig static property added at line 53 — opts scene into LayoutEditorOverlayScene.
